perturbation.original_text: In consideration for the services and facilities provided by you hereunder, Nationwide (on behalf of the Funds) will pay to you, and you will accept as full payment therefore, a fee at the annual rate designated in Appendix A of the average daily net assets of a Fund's Shares owned of record or beneficially by your customers from time to time for which you provide administrative support services hereunder. Such fee will be computed daily and payable monthly by Nationwide within thirty (30) days of receipt from you of a valid invoice (in excel format) that identifies, for each CUSIP of each Fund, the number of sub-accounts, participants or beneficial shareholders serviced by you and such other information as may be necessary to validate the fee payable. The fee rate stated above may be prospectively increased or decreased by the Funds and/or Nationwide, in their sole discretion, at any time upon notice to you. Further, Nationwide or a Fund may, in its sole discretion and without notice, suspend or withdraw the sale of such Shares, including the sale of such Shares to you for the account of any customer(s). Nationwide, in its sole discretion, may arrange for the payment to you of the fees under this Agreement directly by the Funds rather than through Nationwide.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration for the services and facilities provided by you hereunder, Nationwide (on behalf of the Funds) will pay to you, and you will accept as full payment therefore, a fee to be determined. Such fee will be computed daily and payable monthly by Nationwide within thirty (30) days of receipt from you of an invoice. The fee rate may be prospectively increased or decreased by the Funds and/or Nationwide, in their sole discretion, at any time upon notice to you. Further, Nationwide or a Fund may, in its sole discretion and without notice, suspend or withdraw the sale of such Shares, including the sale of such Shares to you for the account of any customer(s). Nationwide, in its sole discretion, may arrange for the payment to you of the fees under this Agreement directly by the Funds rather than through Nationwide.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the specific reference to 'Appendix A' and precise details on how the fee is calculated (annual rate, average daily net assets, excel format, specific information requirements). This creates uncertainty about how the fee is determined and validated. While the agreement states 'a fee to be determined,' it provides no clear method for doing so, leading to potential disputes and difficulty in enforcement. The omission causes a direct in-text contradiction by creating uncertainty about the fee structure when other sections still imply a more concrete arrangement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: APPENDIX A TO SERVICING AGREEMENT Nationwide Mutual Funds Funds of Nationwide Mutual Funds Class R6 and Institutional Service Class shares of all Funds of Nationwide Mutual Funds as offered in the Fund's current prospectus. Administrative Services Fees Share Class Rate (Bps) Institutional Service Class 25 Class R6 0 (no fee) Acknowledgement: Servicing Agent: Nationwide Fund Management LLC 1000 Continental Drive Suite 400 King of Prussia, PA 19406 x x By: By:
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific fee details previously tied to Appendix A, stating instead 'a fee to be determined.' This directly contradicts Appendix A, which provides a concrete fee schedule based on share class rates (Institutional Service Class 25 Bps, Class R6 0). The original text referenced the 'annual rate designated in Appendix A,' creating a clear and specific fee calculation method. By removing this reference and stating only 'a fee to be determined,' the agreement now conflicts with the fixed fee structure outlined in Appendix A. This creates a significant contradiction regarding how the service fee is determined and validated, rendering the agreement ambiguous and potentially unenforceable with respect to fee calculation.

perturbation.original_text: You agree to provide all such information as is reasonably necessary for the Board of Trustees of the Trust to review, at least quarterly, a written report of the amounts of compensation received by you hereunder and the services provided for which you received such compensation. In addition, you will furnish Nationwide with such information as Nationwide or the Funds may reasonably request (including, without limitation, periodic certifications confirming the provision to customers of some or all of the services described herein), and will otherwise cooperate with Nationwide and the Funds (including, without limitation, any auditors designated by the Funds), in connection with the preparation of reports to the Trust's Board of Trustees concerning this Agreement and the monies paid or payable by Nationwide on behalf of the Funds pursuant hereto, as well as any other reports or filings that may be required by law. You agree, upon the reasonable request of Nationwide, to provide access during normal business hours to your facilities and records related to the services provided and the compensation payable hereunder, and to permit Nationwide to review the quality of such services provided and to respond to requests of the Trust's Board of Trustees. Nationwide agrees that all records obtained in connection with access to your facilities is your property and to maintain the confidentiality thereof. In particular, Nationwide agrees that no person having access to such records may use such records or information to solicit, directly or indirectly, any of your customers for any purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: You agree to provide all such information as is reasonably necessary for the Board of Trustees of the Trust to review reports, and will otherwise cooperate with Nationwide and the Funds (including, without limitation, any auditors designated by the Funds), in connection with the preparation of reports to the Trust's Board of Trustees concerning this Agreement, as well as any other reports or filings that may be required by law. You agree, upon the reasonable request of Nationwide, to provide access during normal business hours to your facilities and records related to the services provided, and to permit Nationwide to review the quality of such services provided and to respond to requests of the Trust's Board of Trustees. Nationwide agrees that all records obtained in connection with access to your facilities is your property and to maintain the confidentiality thereof. In particular, Nationwide agrees that no person having access to such records may use such records or information to solicit, directly or indirectly, any of your customers for any purpose.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement for 'a written report of the amounts of compensation received by you hereunder and the services provided for which you received such compensation' and some of the specifics about providing information, the contract now lacks a defined mechanism for oversight of the Servicing Agent's compensation. This creates an internal contradiction because while the agreement requires cooperation and reporting, it eliminates the specific reporting on compensation, making it difficult to verify the accuracy of payments. This contrasts with Section 6, which establishes a fee structure (although now weakened) and implies a need for verifiable compensation data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration for the services and facilities provided by you hereunder, Nationwide (on behalf of the Funds) will pay to you, and you will accept as full payment therefore, a fee to be determined. Such fee will be computed daily and payable monthly by Nationwide within thirty (30) days of receipt from you of an invoice. The fee rate may be prospectively increased or decreased by the Funds and/or Nationwide, in their sole discretion, at any time upon notice to you. Further, Nationwide or a Fund may, in its sole discretion and without notice, suspend or withdraw the sale of such Shares, including the sale of such Shares to you for the account of any customer(s). Nationwide, in its sole discretion, may arrange for the payment to you of the fees under this Agreement directly by the Funds rather than through Nationwide. Quarterly and Other Reports 2 You agree to provide all such information as is reasonably necessary for the Board of Trustees of the Trust to review reports, and will otherwise cooperate with Nationwide and the Funds (including, without limitation, any auditors designated by the Funds), in connection with the preparation of reports to the Trust's Board of Trustees concerning this Agreement, as well as any other reports or filings that may be required by law. You agree, upon the reasonable request of Nationwide, to provide access during normal business hours to your facilities and records related to the services provided, and to permit Nationwide to review the quality of such services provided and to respond to requests of the Trust's Board of Trustees. Nationwide agrees that all records obtained in connection with access to your facilities is your property and to maintain the confidentiality thereof. In particular, Nationwide agrees that no person having access to such records may use such records or information to solicit, directly or indirectly, any of your customers for any purpose.
perturbation.justification: The omission of the requirement for a 'written report of the amounts of compensation received by you hereunder and the services provided for which you received such compensation' creates a contradiction because it removes the specific mechanism for verifying the fee as determined in the first part of the 'Contradicted Text': 'In consideration for the services and facilities provided by you hereunder, Nationwide (on behalf of the Funds) will pay to you, and you will accept as full payment therefore, a fee to be determined.' Without the specific report, the fee determination becomes unverifiable, creating a direct conflict with the intent to accurately compensate the Servicing Agent.

perturbation.original_text: Requests must set forth a specific period, not to exceed 12 months from the date of the request, for which transaction information is sought. A Fund may request transaction information older than 12 months from the date of the request as it deems necessary to investigate compliance with policies established by the Funds for the purpose of eliminating or reducing any dilution of the value of the outstanding shares issued by a Fund.
perturbation.changed_text: Requests must set forth a period for which transaction information is sought. A Fund may request transaction information as it deems necessary to investigate compliance with policies established by the Funds for the purpose of eliminating or reducing any dilution of the value of the outstanding shares issued by a Fund.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'not to exceed 12 months from the date of the request', the modified contract creates uncertainty around the period for which transaction information can be requested. While the original agreement limited this to 12 months, the removal allows the fund to request data from any period. This creates a contradiction with 18.1 Agreement to Provide Information, which specifies the need for taxpayer identification number and registration representative details for transactions which are tied to specific shareholders, which becomes less relevant without any time constraint, leading to ambiguous enforcement and possible overreach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 18. Shareholder Information 18.1 Agreement to Provide Information. You agree to provide the Funds, upon written request, the taxpayer identification number ("TIN"), if known, of any or all Shareholder(s) of an account and the amount, date, name or other identifier of any registered representative(s) associated with the Shareholder(s) or account (if known), and transaction type (purchase, redemption, transfer, or exchange) of every purchase, redemption, transfer, or exchange of Shares held through an account maintained by you during the period covered by the request.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly limits transaction information requests to a 12-month period. The changed text removes this limitation, allowing requests for data from any period. This contradicts section 18.1, which details the specific information required for each transaction (TIN, representative details, transaction type). While the information is still useful, the removal of the time limit makes the scope of the request potentially unbounded and less tied to specific shareholder activities or periods of concern as suggested by the original text, which creates uncertainty and a potential for overreach, making enforcement ambiguous and leading to a direct conflict, specifically by omitting the 'not to exceed 12 months from the date of the request' clause.
